steps.title: Target the claim to refute
steps.content: The student needs to refute the claim that Storting malapportionment favors small urban districts. The notes say the seat formula awards more points for land than for residents, so less populated rural districts with large land areas are overrepresented instead, not the claimed urban districts as the claim says.

steps.title: Reject opposite and vague choices
steps.content: A says rural districts are underrepresented, contradicting the notes. B says more populated districts are overrepresented, also contradicting the notes and reinforcing the wrong direction. C only defines malapportionment and does not address who benefits in Norway's Storting formula. The answer is D.
